Alright, so if you're looking for quality applications, testimonial videos tend to be the best. Obviously you guys have seen it. With my business, the majority of the ads that we run are testimonial based. And so one of the biggest things with testimonials is that you should be continually getting more and more of them. And as long as you're integrating them as a process within your business, it's kind of like an unlimited amount of creative that is already made for you that your customers are happy to make for you. Now having done this a lot, I've a lot of knowledge and I've spent a lot of money on testimonial advertisements. And so we have boiled this one down to a very like, like this is how it works. And I want to walk you through just three examples, but I'm going to go a little bit deeper into them. Alright, so this is the Epiphany Bridge simplified. So there's different versions where people have printed them. This is mine. So the way that you can think about this, and this is how I explained to the video team, this is how I explained it to everyone, is that big picture, you have internal, external in the beginning of their journey, you have the thought process that got them over the bridge to make the decision to work with you, and then internal, external at the end of their journey where they're at now. Alright, so that's just the big picture. It's internal, external at the beginning, the thought process that led them over the bridge and made them say yes, and then internal, external at the end. Alright, and so you can see the little, the three lines, the three arrows there on the left, that's kind of the big picture thought process, and then obviously you want to exit with a call to action, but it shouldn't be too confusing. Now after digging into the videos that have done the best even more, I added a couple of nuances that I think made them even more effective. And so they'll start with the external deficits, external and internal meaning, external is what the outside person might see. So that might be the stats, that might be their actual weight, that might be their clothing size, it's the stats, it's what everyone can recognize and understand. The internal is how they feel about those stats, and what they were unable to do because of this pain that they were suffering, or the negative impacts that they thought were going to occur if they continued down that path. So all of those are, this is the stats, here's how I feel about it and what I couldn't do because of it, pain. Then new opportunity arrives, this is what I saw, this is how I experienced it, this was my doubt, these were the doubts and the skepticism that I had prior deciding to move forward, and then the thing that ultimately made me move forward was the person was like, you at your gym were super friendly, you were super knowledgeable, you arrived on time, this is actually really good information for you anyways to mind because you can find out what are the key triggers that actually got people to say yes. But the reason we have them explain that process in the video is because we want the user, we want the person who's watching or the prospect to go along that same journey with them, so that they relate to where they're at now and then they follow the thought process of an ad that they're seeing just like the one they're seeing right now and then they see, oh, I could have this feeling like this person has and then that's what makes them ultimately click. And so in the list here it's external deficits, internal doubts, fears and pains, that's one and two, three, four, five is what they saw, what their doubts were, what made them say yes, and then six, seven is external achievements, I lost this much weight, you don't want to put a duration in there because then you can get flagged for compliance but I lost this much weight, I dropped this many inches, I have this dress size, things that people understand, but internally the transformation, now I think about food completely differently, I feel like I'm free when I go to my family events, I don't stress, I'm still eating pizza and losing weight and honestly I feel like I'm in control again. So you want to say the transformation that's occurred and what they can do and feel now as a result of that transformation. And then finally the last is number eight is if you're on the fence and you're in the same shoes I was or maybe you're better off than I was, you should definitely take the leap of faith and click and sign up here because it'll change your life. Alright, so boom that's the Epiphany Bridge, that is how it works and so what I want to lead you through is three, or here's two of our most successful ads that use that same bridge and then I'm going to show you a professional version of the same thing. If you're going to do like an interview style, I'd recommend you ask them the question and then they should repeat the question before answering it. It just works better if you're trying to cut a video. But on a basic level, if you don't want to do any kind of that production value, this raw style works insanely well. And it's not like the professional really does any better than the other stuff. I just have the people until I do it. But this works just as well, if not better than the professional stuff. You just want to give people an idea that this is what you want them to talk about. And the underlined points are going to be two and seven. Those are the big pieces. And you just want to make sure that they just mention what made them make the decision, what made them take the jump, and then spend the rest of the time just kind of half pitching someone on why they should do it and why they should put their fears away. So that's the testimonial videos done correctly. And that is what's going to generate higher quality applications, which is why my business, which is majority applications, actually it's 100% applications, is based on these style ads and the following ad that I'm going to show you.
